principles: Separate provenance from interpretation: who posted it, where it came from, what kind of content it is, and what business insight it supports are different labels.
Do not label official, employee, or partner marketing as user pain or a feature request unless the source directly quotes or summarizes user feedback.
Cluster labels should be evidence-grounded. Absence of detail in a launch post is not by itself a user request.
Gold eval cases should preserve raw evidence, source IDs, URLs, sender role, source group, and current production labels so regressions are traceable.

labels.official_brand: Company-owned account, website, docs, blog, YouTube, LinkedIn page, or official launch channel.
labels.founder_exec: Founder, CEO, executive, or senior public company representative.
labels.employee: Employee or team member speaking from a personal account.
labels.affiliate_creator: Creator, educator, partner, ambassador, or friendly third party with clear affiliation or amplification relationship.
labels.customer_user: Named customer, user, practitioner, or buyer speaking from direct usage.
labels.independent_practitioner: Independent technical practitioner, analyst, blogger, or community member without clear affiliation.
labels.analyst_media: Media, analyst, newsletter, review site, or third-party publication.
labels.community_member: Forum/social participant without known company affiliation.
labels.unknown: Insufficient metadata to identify the speaker.

labels.owned_official: Official company-controlled marketing, docs, product, or content surfaces.
labels.affiliated_insider: Employees, founders, executives, or close affiliates.
labels.competitor_ecosystem: Competitor official, employee, partner, benchmark, or comparison content.
labels.organic_market: Unaffiliated users, buyers, practitioners, HN/Reddit discussions, and general market conversations.
labels.creator_analyst: Creators, educators, analysts, newsletters, or media that can influence demand.
labels.customer_proof: Customer stories, case studies, testimonials, or direct success evidence.

content_type.description: Primary communicative form of the source.

labels.product_launch: Announcement of availability, beta, SDK, model support, integration, pricing, or product capability.
labels.technical_proof: Benchmark, performance claim, architecture detail, reliability proof, latency/throughput result, or eval result.
labels.customer_success: Customer win, case study, testimonial, usage result, deployment outcome, or proof point.
labels.tutorial_how_to: Setup guide, walkthrough, coding-agent instructions, docs, or practical implementation path.
labels.competitive_claim: Comparison against another provider/model/platform or explicit competitive positioning.
labels.thought_leadership: Category framing, vision, market point of view, or executive narrative.
labels.event_community: Conference, livestream, webinar, hackathon, community activation, award, or brand event.
labels.partner_integration: Partner, ecosystem integration, marketplace, or co-marketing announcement.
labels.user_review_experience: User or buyer report of actual experience, usage, adoption, confusion, praise, or objection.
labels.support_question: Question asking how to use, fix, evaluate, or connect something.
labels.bug_report: Report of a failure, incorrect behavior, outage, broken flow, or implementation problem.
labels.feature_request_post: Direct ask for a missing capability, integration, UX improvement, or roadmap item.
labels.pricing_procurement: Cost, budget, procurement, contract, usage pricing, or credits discussion.
labels.hiring_corporate: Hiring, funding, company building, financial, or corporate update.
labels.generic_reaction: Low-detail reaction, praise, repost, or short comment with little standalone signal.
labels.off_topic: Not substantively about Fireworks or the target market.

feedback_type.description: Multi-label only when the source contains real market/user feedback.

labels.pain_point: User/customer expresses friction, failure, cost burden, confusion, risk, or workflow breakage.
labels.feature_request: User/customer asks for a capability, integration, documentation, workflow, or product change.
labels.buying_objection: Evaluation concern that can block purchase/adoption.
labels.comparison_or_switching: Comparison, shortlist, migration, or switching discussion.
labels.workflow_gap: Glue work, manual steps, setup burden, or process breakdown.
labels.performance_reliability: Latency, throughput, reliability, uptime, scaling, or correctness concern.
labels.docs_guidance_request: Need for clearer docs, examples, setup instructions, or guidance.
labels.positive_testimonial: Positive direct usage signal or endorsement.
labels.success_metric: Specific outcome, metric, or improvement from usage.
labels.question_request: Open question without clear complaint or feature ask.

insight_category.description: Business interpretation assigned to a cluster or synthesized insight.

labels.pain_point: A recurring problem or friction users are experiencing.
labels.feature_request: Repeated or important asks for missing product capability or workflow support.
labels.opportunity: Evidence-grounded market gap, buyer pull, or topic Fireworks should act on.
labels.competitive: Competitor comparison, competitive positioning, or benchmark fight.
labels.visibility: Awareness/discoverability gap or place Fireworks should show up.
labels.marketing_narrative: Owned or affiliated messaging, category framing, community momentum, or brand positioning.
labels.product_launch: Launch or availability story that should not be mistaken for feedback.
labels.technical_proof: Performance, reliability, architecture, or benchmark evidence.
labels.customer_success: Customer proof, case study, testimonial, or outcome evidence.
labels.off_topic: Not useful for the Fireworks marketing/eval task.

labels.keep: Cluster is coherent and category/title are acceptable.
labels.rename: Evidence is coherent but title/category/framing is wrong or too vague.
labels.split: Evidence mixes distinct themes, source roles, or claims that should be separate clusters.
labels.merge: Cluster duplicates another cluster and should be merged.
labels.discard: Cluster is off-topic, too low-signal, or unsupported.

failure_mode.description: Known failures this eval should catch.

labels.official_marketing_as_feedback: Official/employee marketing was mislabeled as user pain or feature request.
labels.unsupported_inference: Insight infers a claim not supported by evidence.
labels.absence_as_evidence: Missing detail in evidence was treated as proof of demand.
labels.mixed_cluster: Cluster combines distinct topics or source roles.
labels.vague_title: Title is generic, copied, truncated, or not actionable.
labels.source_role_blindness: Insight fails to distinguish who said what.
labels.entity_attribution_error: A claim is attributed to the wrong company or speaker.
labels.mixed_entity_cluster: Cluster combines target and competitor claims without separating entities.
labels.competitor_marketing_as_market_feedback: Competitor-owned marketing is treated as independent user or market evidence.
labels.unsupported_comparative_claim: Insight claims one company wins or loses without direct comparative evidence.
labels.winner_claim_without_independent_evidenc: Insight declares a winner using only vendor-owned claims.
labels.low_signal: Evidence lacks enough substance for an insight.
labels.duplicate_theme: Same theme appears in multiple clusters.
